# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 441
- **Lines of code**: 112,407
- **Languages**: TypeScript (356), JSON (26), CSS (19), Other (13), JavaScript (12)
- **Zones**: 12 (12 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-09T16:51:42.575Z

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

### Root (`root`)

4 files, primarily JavaScript

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/root/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/login → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_auth.login.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Token budget and usage tracking logic duplicated across lifecycle files instead of centralized service [warning] (agent-core)
- Zone mixes three distinct architectural layers: analysis (review, summary), planning (brief, context), and lifecycle (loops, token management) that should be separate zones [warning] (agent-core)
- CLI commands import packages/hench/src/agent/lifecycle/loop.ts directly, violating layer separation and creating tight coupling [critical] (cli-infrastructure)
- Error handling logic embedded in CLI layer instead of using domain service abstractions [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- CLI commands bypass service layer to directly manipulate agent lifecycle internals, violating clean architecture principles [critical]
- Multiple bidirectional coupling patterns between hench zones (hench↔hench-3, hench-3↔hench-4, hench-4↔hench-5) create circular dependency chains that complicate module loading and increase risk of deadlocks [critical]
- Token management logic distributed across agent-core lifecycle files and task-completion validation without centralized abstraction [warning]
- Tool ecosystem lacks standardized interface contract between security validation and tool implementation layers, creating fragile coupling through implicit assumptions about validation behavior [warning]
- Each specialized tool implements its own command parsing and execution without shared abstractions, leading to inconsistent interfaces and duplicated validation logic [warning] (specialized-tools)
- Critical architectural debt: circular dependency chains (hench zones) + hub topology (rex.ts) + dependency inversion violations (CLI→agent-core) form triple threat to module loading stability [critical]
- ... and 1 more

### Patterns

- Clean adapter pattern implementation allowing swappable Claude API providers while maintaining consistent client interface [critical] (claude-integration)
- Clear layered architecture with orchestration layer at top, CLI infrastructure in middle, and agent core at bottom provides good separation of concerns [info]
- Tool ecosystem demonstrates proper layered security architecture with no privilege escalation paths [info]
- Implements facade pattern with four distinct entry points (ci, cli, config, web) that coordinate package interactions without creating dependencies [info] (orchestration-layer)
- Command pattern with validation pipeline provides robust task completion workflow with proper state management [info] (task-completion)
- Security zone properly gates all tool access without creating backdoors or escape hatches [info] (tool-security)
- Agent subsystem violates dependency inversion principle - high-level modules (cli-infrastructure, orchestration-layer) depend directly on low-level modules (agent-core) instead of abstractions [critical]
- Zone naming inconsistency reveals architectural confusion - mixing domain concepts (task-completion) with technical layers (cli-infrastructure) indicates unclear separation of concerns [warning]

### Relationships

- Heavy bidirectional coupling with cli-infrastructure (30 total imports) suggests agent logic bleeding into CLI concerns [warning] (agent-core)
- Bidirectional coupling with agent-core may indicate CLI commands directly manipulating agent internals instead of using clean interfaces [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- Clear separation between security concerns (tool-security) and functionality (specialized-tools) with well-defined interfaces [info]
- Hub-and-spoke pattern centered on packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts creates single point of dependency across multiple zones [warning]
- Missing interface abstraction between agent-core and cli-infrastructure zones leads to tight coupling through direct imports [warning]
- Clean consumer relationship with tool-security zone - accepts validation without coupling to implementation [info] (specialized-tools)

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-client)
- Bidirectional coupling: "hench" ↔ "hench-3" (9+21 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-3" ↔ "hench-4" (4+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-4" ↔ "hench-5" (2+3 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 27 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (hench)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (root)
- Well-architected agent core with strong cohesion and manageable coupling [critical] (agent-core)
- Exemplary client library design with perfect cohesion and encapsulation [info] (claude-integration)
- ... and 14 more

### Suggestions

- Analysis subfolder implements post-execution concerns mixed with core logic - separate into analysis-reporting zone [warning] (agent-core)
- Token management files in lifecycle/ subfolder violate single responsibility - extract to dedicated resource-management zone [warning] (agent-core)
- token-usage.ts mixing client concerns with analytics creates implicit service responsibility - consider extracting to dedicated analytics zone [info] (claude-integration)
- CLI importing agent/lifecycle/loop.ts creates architectural layer violation - implement service interface to decouple CLI from agent internals [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- cli/errors.ts contains domain-specific error types rather than infrastructure errors - creates tight coupling between presentation and business layers [warning] (cli-infrastructure)
- commands/constants.ts mixing configuration with command logic - extract to centralized configuration management [info] (cli-infrastructure)
- Add tool execution session management to track validation failures and implement progressive restrictions for tools that repeatedly fail security checks [info]
- Consider bypassing dispatch layer for tools that require no external resources (like pure computation tools) to reduce serialization overhead [info]
- Create centralized resource-management zone to consolidate token budget, usage tracking, and other resource concerns scattered across agent-core and task-completion [warning]
- Establish consistent zone naming convention - current zones mix functional (task-completion) and architectural (cli-infrastructure) naming styles [info]
- ... and 16 more
